title: Combination Cooking Methods
content:
COMBINATION METHODS

Fireless Cookery: Preparing food in heavily insulated container. Cooked foods must be thoroughly heated before placing in well. Chilled foods require packing in 4 parts ice to 1 part salt.

Waterless Cookery: Cooking slowly in tightly covered container with minimal (¼-½ cup) water to start process.

Blanching: Cooking briefly (3-5 minutes) in boiling water to whiten, loosen skins, or set color. For vegetables to be frozen or canned, followed by ice water plunge.

Parboiling: Partial cooking in boiling water. Used to set color, partially dehydrate, or prepare for final cooking.

Wrap Cookery: Cooking food wrapped in leaves (cabbage, grape, banana), parchment, or foil. Creates steaming effect. Foil is impervious and traps moisture; paper allows some steam escape.

HIGH-ALTITUDE ADJUSTMENTS

Water boils at lower temperatures as altitude increases:
- Sea level: 212°F
- 5,000 ft: 203°F
- 10,000 ft: 194°F

For baking: Decrease leavening slightly, increase liquid, and/or raise oven temperature 10-15°F. For pressure cooking: Maintain same timing but increase pressure ½ lb per 1,000 ft elevation. Roasting procedures remain essentially unchanged.
